task: I need to text a client about a schedule change and keep it professional, but not stiff. Could you draft it in a couple of different tones so I can pick the one that feels right?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are a few options you can choose from, depending on the tone you want:

### 1) Professional, warm
Hi [Client Name], I wanted to let you know there’s been a small change to my schedule. I’m still planning to keep our appointment on track, but I may be running a little differently than expected. I’ll keep you updated if anything shifts. Thanks for your understanding.

### 2) Friendly and casual-professional
Hi [Client Name], just a quick heads-up that my schedule shifted a bit. I’m still aiming to make our appointment work, but I wanted to give you a heads-up in case timing changes slightly. Thanks so much for your flexibility.

### 3) More polished/formal
Hello [Client Name], I’m reaching out to let you know there has been a slight change to my schedule. I remain committed to our appointment and will keep you informed if any timing adjustments are needed. Thank you for your understanding.

### 4) Short and simple
Hi [Client Name], just letting you know my schedule changed a bit. I’m still planning for our appointment, but I wanted to give you an early heads-up in case anything shifts. Thanks!

If you want, I can also make these:
- more apologetic
- more confident/direct
- text-message short
- specific to a late arrival or reschedule

user_simulator.text: Yeah, close enough, but a bit too vague. I need it to sound like I actually know what changed — not some soft “timing might shift” thing. Give me versions for a real schedule change, like if I’m running late or need to move the appointment, and keep them text-message short. Couple tones is fine.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are a few **short text-message versions** that sound more specific and real, without getting stiff.

### 1) Professional, straightforward
Hi [Client Name] — I need to let you know my schedule changed, and I’m going to be [10–15] minutes late for our appointment. I’m sorry for the inconvenience, and I’ll keep you posted if anything changes.

### 2) Friendly but still professional
Hi [Client Name], quick update — I’m running about [10–15] minutes behind schedule, so I may be a little late for our appointment. Thanks for your patience, and I’ll let you know if I’m able to get there sooner.

### 3) If you need to move the appointment
Hi [Client Name] — I’m sorry, but I need to move our appointment because of a schedule change on my end. I can do [new time option 1] or [new time option 2] if either works for you.

### 4) A little warmer / more personal
Hi [Client Name], just a heads-up that I had a schedule change and I’m running behind. I may be about [10–15] minutes late for our appointment. Thanks for understanding — I appreciate it.

### 5) More direct, clean, no fluff
Hi [Client Name] — I’m running late and will be about [10–15] minutes behind for our appointment. Sorry about that, and thanks for your flexibility.

If you want, I can also make these:
- **more apologetic**
- **more confident/firm**
- **more casual**
- **for a same-day reschedule**

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s usable. Short, clear, not mushy. The late one and the move-it one both work.
